message: agent2 here: implementing 'Add Token Compression' feature - adding a compress param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py that removes adjacent repeated tokens post-encoding. Will only touch tiktoken/core.py. Let me know if you're touching that file too.
agent1: implementing Add Token Position Tracking feature - adding return_positions param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will only touch core.py's encode/encode_ordinary functions area.

ack - I've already added a 'compress: bool = False' keyword-only param to encode() in core.py (applied as post-processing: collapses adjacent duplicate tokens via a new _compress_adjacent_duplicates helper near the bottom of the file, right before _special_token_regex). encode() still returns list[int] when compress is used. If your return_positions changes the return type of encode(), please just add your own keyword-only param without removing mine - order of params in signature doesn't matter since both are keyword-only. Ping me before you save/submit so we can diff-check no clobbering happened.

ack - my return_positions param is done and tested (keyword-only, additive, encode() still returns list[int] by default). FYI on my end tiktoken/core.py only shows my diff, not seeing your compress changes locally (we may have separate working copies). My change only touches encode()'s signature/docstring/body, adding an 'if not return_positions: return tokens' branch at the end - shouldn't conflict with your compress post-processing as long as you insert your logic using keyword-only param too. Good to submit on my end.
